summary: Validated record for the official Dynamic Lock policy under Passport for Work. The app now writes the documented machine policy value instead of the older Winlogon preference path.

setting.name: Dynamic Lock
setting.casual_explanation: Dynamic Lock can automatically lock the PC when Windows decides the user is away, often based on Bluetooth signals.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it when they want the PC to lock automatically when they walk away.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Automatic locking can improve safety, but it can also trigger unexpectedly or depend on Bluetooth signal behavior.
setting.professional_notes: Passport.admx documents the official policy at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\PassportForWork\DynamicLock with value name DynamicLock. The local ADML help text says enabling the policy evaluates configured signal rules to detect user absence and lock the device. The app now writes the documented policy path instead of the older current-user Winlogon preference value.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not enforce Dynamic Lock signal rules through this policy.

allowed_values.label: Policy signal rules enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Configured signal rules are evaluated to detect user absence and automatically lock the device.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now writes DynamicLock = 1 on the official Passport for Work policy path.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems supporting the Passport for Work Dynamic Lock policy

states.rationale: Leave the official Dynamic Lock policy unset.

windows_defaults.notes: This record validates the official Passport for Work policy surface used by the app.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
avoid_for: Systems that should enforce Dynamic Lock policy explicitly

states.rationale: Leave the official Dynamic Lock policy unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to keep the official Dynamic Lock policy unset.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to enforce Dynamic Lock signal rules.
tradeoffs: The Dynamic Lock policy stays at the Windows-managed baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Enable Dynamic Lock
intended_for: Users who want automatic lock behavior
Devices with the needed signal setup
avoid_for: Systems that should never lock automatically from Dynamic Lock signal rules

states.rationale: Enable the documented Passport for Work Dynamic Lock policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to evaluate Dynamic Lock signal rules and lock the device when the user is away.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want automatic locking tied to Dynamic Lock signal behavior.
tradeoffs: Lock behavior now depends on the signal rules Windows evaluates.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Passport.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the official Dynamic Lock policy to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\PassportForWork\DynamicLock with value name DynamicLock and enabledValue 1.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Passport.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy evaluates configured signal rules to detect user absence and automatically lock the device.

decision.why: The official Dynamic Lock policy surface is validated in this record, and the app now writes the same documented policy path and value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: research/evidence-files/external/c/Windows/PolicyDefinitions/Passport.admx: policy name="MSPassport_UseDynamicLock" key="SOFTWARE/Policies/Microsoft/PassportForWork/DynamicLock" valueName="DynamicLock" enabledValue=1; research/evidence-files/external/c/PolicyDefinitions/en-US/Passport.adml: "If you enable this policy setting, these signal rules will be evaluated to detect user absence and automatically lock the device."
validation_proof.notes: The official Dynamic Lock policy surface is machine-checkably documented in the local ADMX and ADML files.
